title: Character Creation Step 6: Alignment & Deity
description: Defines options for selecting character's moral alignment and deity (optional) in Pathfinder 2E character creation

alignmentOption.description: Individual alignment choice with ID, name, and description

examples.name: Lawful Good
examples.description: Acts with compassion and honor within the bounds of law and order.

examples.name: Neutral
examples.description: Avoids moral extremes and seeks balance or personal interest.

description.description: Explanation of the alignment's moral and ethical perspective

deityOption.description: Deity choice with stats and portfolio information

examples.name: Iomedae
examples.description: Goddess of justice, honor, and righteous valor

examples.name: No Deity
examples.description: Character does not worship a specific deity

alignment.description: Deity's alignment (must match alignmentOption.id enum or 'Any')

description.description: Brief description of deity's portfolio and role

step.description: Step number in character creation process

step_description.const: Define the values that guide your character's decisions across the full life of a campaign.
step_description.description: Explanation of what happens in this step

fields.description: Form fields available in this step

options.description: Available alignments in Pathfinder 2E (all nine standard alignments)

default.name: Lawful Good
default.description: Acts with compassion and honor within the bounds of law and order.

default.name: Neutral Good
default.description: Does good without bias toward or against order, helping others as needed.

default.name: Chaotic Good
default.description: Acts with freedom and kindness, following conscience over rules.

default.name: Lawful Neutral
default.description: Values tradition, order, and hierarchy above morality.

default.name: Neutral
default.description: Avoids moral extremes and seeks balance or personal interest.

default.name: Chaotic Neutral
default.description: Follows individual freedom and impulse without regard for tradition or others.

default.name: Lawful Evil
default.description: Uses tradition and order as tools for exploitation and control.

default.name: Neutral Evil
default.description: Acts selfishly with no regard for others, unbound by law or chaos.

default.name: Chaotic Evil
default.description: Driven by greed, hatred, or bloodlust with no restraint or order.

help_text.const: Use alignment as a roleplay compass for long-term consistency, not a rigid rule that blocks character growth.

class_restrictions.description: Some classes restrict alignment choices (e.g., Champions must follow their deity's alignment)

champion.description: Champions must follow alignments compatible with their deity

error_message.const: Please select an alignment before continuing.

deity.description: Deity selection field (optional for most classes, required for clerics and champions)

label.const: Choose a Deity or Guiding Belief (Required for Clerics & Champions)

required.description: Optional for most classes, but validation enforces requirement for clerics and champions

examples.name: Iomedae
examples.description: Goddess of justice, honor, and righteous valor

examples.name: Sarenrae
examples.description: Goddess of healing, redemption, and the sun

examples.name: Desna
examples.description: Goddess of dreams, stars, travelers, and luck

examples.name: Abadar
examples.description: God of cities, law, merchants, and wealth

examples.name: Gozreh
examples.description: Deity of nature, the sea, and weather

examples.name: Cayden Cailean
examples.description: God of freedom, ale, wine, and bravery

examples.name: No Deity
examples.description: Character does not worship a specific deity

help_text.const: Clerics and Champions must choose a deity, as their powers come from divine worship. Other classes may choose to worship a deity for roleplay purposes.

required_for_classes.description: Classes that must choose a deity (clerics and champions)

alignment_compatibility.description: When enabled, enforces Pathfinder 2E rule: Character alignment must be within one step of deity alignment on both the Law-Chaos axis and Good-Evil axis (e.g., a Neutral Good deity allows LG, NG, CG, LN, N, CN followers).

error_message.const: Clerics and Champions must choose a deity. Your alignment must be within one step of your deity's alignment.

can_go_back.description: User can return to previous step

validation.description: Step-level validation rules enforced before allowing navigation to next step

required_fields.description: Fields that must be completed before advancing

conditional_requirements.description: Additional requirements based on previous choices

deity_required_for_classes.description: Classes that must choose a deity

error_messages.description: User-facing error messages for validation failures

alignment_missing.const: Please select an alignment before continuing.

deity_required.const: Clerics and Champions must choose a deity.

alignment_incompatible.const: Your alignment must be within one step of your deity's alignment.

tips.$comment: Steps 4-8 use simple string tips for brevity, while steps 1-3 use structured objects with title/text properties
default: Choose a worldview you can roleplay consistently over many sessions.
Party trust and long-term campaign cohesion usually benefit from cooperative alignments.
Deity choice can provide strong identity anchors for divine and conviction-driven characters.
If your character evolves, alignment can evolve too—treat it as living narrative guidance.
Use this step to define why your character stays in the world long enough to become a legend.
